items.title: Susan Blackmore - How Belief Systems Affect Believers (Part 1)
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Get access to over 5,000 videos by signing up for a free Closer To Truth membership: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Why do people believe the things they do? We like to think that we are rational, that what we believe is true. But often we come to our beliefs, unknowingly, as a product of our particular ‘belief systems’. How do belief systems work their credulous magic? What principles enable belief systems to influence individuals and commandeer groups?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Susan Jane Blackmore is a writer, lecturer, and broadcaster and a visiting professor at the University of Plymouth. Her fields of research include memetics, parapsychology, consciousness, and she is best known for her book "The Meme Machine". 

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

items.title: Paul Griffiths - Philosophy of Sex, Sexuality, & Gender
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Paul Griffiths is Professor of Philosophy at the University of Sydney and works primarily in the Philosophy of Science and more particularly Philosophy of Biology.

items.title: C. Stephen Evans - What is Virtue in a Theistic World?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Get access to over 5,000 videos by signing up for a free Closer To Truth membership: https://closertotruth.com/register/

What is the deep nature of virtue? If God exists, then what is virtue? Does virtue differ in possible worlds where God exists and where God does not exist? How fundamental is virtue?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

C. Stephen Evans is Emeritus Professor of Philosophy and Humanities at Baylor University. He is one of the United States' leading experts on Søren Kierkegaard.

items.title: Pascal Boyer - Aesthetic Cognitivism II: Intellectual Foundations
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on X for videos, articles, and polls on life's biggest questions: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

Aesthetic Cognitivism is a theory about the arts as sources of understanding. What are the scholarly or scientific fields that form its foundation? Explore art’s relationship with cognitive science, philosophy, neuroscience, history, theology and religion.

Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Pascal Robert Boyer is a Franco-American cognitive anthropologist and evolutionary psychologist, mostly known for his work in the cognitive science of religion. He is a Henry Luce Professor of Individual and Collective Memory at Washington University in St. Louis, Departments of Psychology and Anthropology, where he teaches classes on evolutionary psychology and anthropology.

items.title: Philip Kitcher - Philosophy of Reductionism & Emergence
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Can biology be explained entirely in terms of chemistry and then physics? If so, that’s “reductionism.” Or are there “emergent” properties at higher levels of the hierarchy of life that cannot be explained by properties at lower or more basic levels?

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Philip Stuart Kitcher is a British philosopher who is the John Dewey Professor Emeritus of philosophy at Columbia University. He specialises in the philosophy of science, the philosophy of biology, the philosophy of mathematics, and more recently pragmatism.

items.title: Jimo Borjigin - What Do Near-Death Experiences Mean? | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Donate to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Stories of ‘near death experiences’ (NDEs) are legion, affecting all cultures and peoples. NDEs have similarities—white lights, tunnels, spirit beings, feelings of unity, comfort, love. Do NDEs ‘prove’ that ‘Heaven is for real’? Or are they mere by-products of shocks to the brain during times of physiological trauma and insults? Is there a science of NDEs?

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Jimo Borjigin is an Associate Professor of Molecular & Integrative Physiology as well as an Associate Professor of Neurology at the University of Michigan. Borjigin received her Ph.D. in Neuroscience at The Johns Hopkins Medical Institution in Baltimore, MD.

items.title: Liad Mudrik - What is Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow Closer To Truth on X for articles, announcements, viewer polls, and more: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

To study consciousness comprehensively and rigorously, what kinds of data or information are relevant? Data/information for Materialism theories, which are subject to the scientific method, can be well defined. But what about non-Materialism theories?

Get access to over 5,000 videos with a free Closer To Truth membership: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Professor Liad Mudrik is a researcher at the school of psychological sciences and Sagol school of neuroscience at Tel Aviv University. Her research focuses on conscious experience, its neural mechanisms and functions.

items.title: Robert Spitzer - What Is God?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to support Closer To Truth: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

To assess whether God exists, we must ask what kind of God is supposed to exist? But can humans probe God? Can we inquire into God’s essence and nature? Is God at all knowable? Or forever beyond human understanding? What questions might we ask about God? Let’s assume nothing about God, but begin with the simple question, “What is God?”

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Fr. Robert J. Spitzer SJ is an American Jesuit priest, philosopher, educator, author, speaker, and retired President of Gonzaga University in Spokane, Washington.

items.title: Is There Empirical Evidence for Life After Death?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Can consciousness survive physical death?

After immersing himself in the evidence for a year, Imants Barušs concluded that survival may be the most reasonable interpretation.

Watch the full conversation on our channel.</article>

items.title: Susan Schneider - Can AI Become Conscious?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Register for a free Closer To Truth membership and get access to over 5,000 video interviews: https://closertotruth.com/register/

AI consciousness, its possibility or probability, has burst into public debate, eliciting all kinds of issues from AI ethics and rights to AI going rogue and harming humanity. We explore diverse views; we argue that AI consciousness depends on theories of consciousness.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Susan Schneider is an American philosopher and artificial intelligence expert. She is the founding director of the Center for the Future of AI, Mind, & Society at Florida Atlantic University where she also holds the William F. Dietrich Distinguished Professorship. She is also Co-Principal Investigator at the Machine Perception and Cognitive Robotics Laboratory (MPCR Lab) in the Center for Complex Systems and Brain Sciences. Schneider has also held the Baruch S. Blumberg NASA/Library of Congress Chair in Astrobiology, Exploration, and Scientific Innovation at NASA and the Distinguished Scholar Chair at the Library of Congress.

items.title: Ivette Fuentes - Experiments in Quantum Mechanics
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Show your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Quantum theory has been the most powerful explanation of how the world, down deep, really works. But quantum theory is weird: things in two states or places at the same time, probabilities not actualities of events, entanglement over vast distances. How do precise, repeatable experiments support such counterintuitive concepts?

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Ivette Fuentes is a Professor of Quantum Physics at the University of Southampton and Emmy Fellow at Keble College, University of Oxford. Her work considers fundamental quantum mechanics, quantum optics and their interplay with general relativity.

items.title: Christopher Southgate - If God Exists, Why is There Evil?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

When earthquakes and tsunamis kill and maim, when children suffer painful illnesses and die young, where is the God who is supposedly all-powerful and all-good? When humans suffer severely, if the horrors are not caused by other human beings, it is called ‘natural evil’. For the theist, is natural evil more difficult to justify than moral evil?

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Christopher Southgate is Research Fellow in Theology at the University of Exeter, UK.

items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Ecology, environmental protection, and dealing with climate change are among humanity’s most serious problems. Can a philosophical perspective clarify old issues and suggest new solutions?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant and Friedrich Nietzsche.

items.title: Galen Strawson - Idealism: Arguing Pro and Con
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Donate to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Idealism is the theory that all reality is consciousness and only consciousness, and that what we perceive to be the physical world is either a manifestation of consciousness or derived from it. Either way, fundamental reality is pure consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Idealism (like the common sense of the physical)?

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Galen John Strawson is a British analytic philosopher and literary critic who works primarily on philosophy of mind, metaphysics, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant and Friedrich Nietzsche.

items.title: What Would Falling Into a Black Hole Feel Like?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>What would the end of the universe feel like from the inside? Robert Lawrence Kuhn explores the paradox of the Big Crunch and black holes, where time may appear infinite from within, but instantaneous from outside.

Watch the full Ask Me Everything on our channel.</article>
